prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French
Le silence entre nous en disait plus que ne l'aurait fait n'importe quelle dispute.

Note: I used the literary construction "en disait plus que ne l'aurait fait..." to render the figurative "said more than any argument could have." I chose "dispute" (querelle) rather than "discussion" because the English "argument" implies a quarrel.

Japanese
私たちの間の沈黙は、どんな口論よりも多くを語っていた。

Note: I used 沈黙が「語っていた」 (was speaking/telling) to capture the metaphorical "said." I chose 口論 (quarrel) rather than 議論 (discussion/debate) to match the sense of a personal argument.

Arabic
كان صمتنا أبلغَ من أيّ جدالٍ.

Note: I used أبلغ ("more eloquent") instead of a literal "قال أكثر" because it is the more idiomatic way in Arabic to say that silence communicated more effectively than an argument would. I also used صمتنا (our silence) which naturally implies "between us."
